learningObjectives.title: Understand viva response structure
learningObjectives.description: Explain SAP TM concepts with definition, business purpose, practical example, and business value.

learningObjectives.title: Understand end-to-end transportation explanation
learningObjectives.description: Describe full business flow from order creation through settlement and reporting.

learningObjectives.title: Understand interview readiness
learningObjectives.description: Apply scenario-based answers across core SAP TM objects and integration topics.

sections.content: The SAP TM Viva evaluates conceptual understanding, business process knowledge and implementation awareness rather than memorization. Candidates are expected to explain transportation processes, SAP TM objects, integrations and real-world business scenarios clearly and confidently.

sections.title: Viva Strategy
sections.content: Always answer in three steps: Define the concept → Explain its business purpose → Give a practical example.

sections.title: Core SAP TM Viva Questions
rows: Transportation planning, execution and freight settlement solution.
Optimize transportation cost, resources and delivery performance.
ERP manages business transactions; TM manages transportation.
Planning object created from transportation demand.

sections.title: Interview Examples
rows: A planning recommendation suggesting the best transportation option before Freight Order creation.
To choose the most suitable carrier based on cost, service level and business rules.
Managing vehicles, drivers and transportation equipment used during execution.
Why integrate SAP TM with EWM?
To synchronize warehouse execution with transportation planning.

sections.content: Suppose a customer places an order. Explain how SAP TM handles it. The expected answer is: Sales Order creates an Outbound Delivery, which generates a Freight Unit. Planning creates a Transportation Proposal and then a Freight Order. The shipment is executed, monitored and finally settled with the carrier through Freight Settlement.

sections.title: Best Practice
sections.content: Whenever asked 'What is X?', answer using this format: Definition → Purpose → Example → Business Benefit.

sections.title: Example Viva Answer
sections.content: Question: What is a Freight Unit? Answer: A Freight Unit is the basic planning object in SAP TM. It is created from transportation demand such as an outbound delivery. The planning engine groups Freight Units into Freight Orders for execution, helping optimize transportation resources and costs.

sections.title: Exam Tip
sections.content: Most viva questions focus on business flow rather than configuration. Be able to explain the complete transportation process confidently.

sections.title: Common Mistake
sections.content: Avoid giving one-word answers. Interviewers evaluate understanding, communication and logical explanation.

items: Know all major SAP TM objects.
